title: Discipline Support Officer (School)
description:
Job Summary

The Discipline Support Officer will support student discipline and welfare for Secondary 1 to Secondary 5 students by conducting investigations, supervising detention and suspension activities, and promoting restorative practices to foster student growth and positive behaviour.

Responsibilities

  • Conduct preliminary investigations and fact-finding interviews to gather student and witness statements, compile evidence, and prepare structured incident reports to inform key personnel decision-making
  • Supervise daily recess, lunch, and detention sessions (Monday to Thursday), maintaining accurate attendance and behavioural records during detention
  • Coordinate reflection and restitution activities with Student Management committee members to facilitate student understanding of choices, consequences, and improvement strategies
  • Design and implement meaningful detention tasks that support student development and behavioural improvement
  • Establish a structured and supportive environment for students under in-house suspension to maintain learning continuity and guide reflective and re-entry support activities
  • Support lower secondary recess dismissal routines and serve as a visible adult presence in high-traffic campus areas to promote safety and discipline
  • Conduct systematic patrols during recess, lunch breaks, and lesson transitions to proactively manage student behaviour and support discipline strategies
  • Assist in peer reconciliation or mediation efforts to resolve conflicts constructively and promote positive student relationships
  • Collaborate effectively with teachers and Student Management personnel to uphold confidentiality, procedural fairness, and professional standards
  • Demonstrate professionalism, discretion, sound judgement, and respectful engagement with students to foster a positive and supportive school environment

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Former working experience (preferably more than 10 years) in uniformed service background (Police, Prison Service or equivalent) with involvement in youth-related case investigation and restorative work such as behavioural support
  • Conflict management and de-escalation skills applied in youth or educational settings
  • Ability to perform calm and proportionate behavioural interventions to manage challenging student behaviours
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build rapport with students while upholding behavioural expectations
  • Accurate incident observation and documentation skills to support thorough reporting and follow-up
  • Firm yet respectful approach when working with students to balance discipline with care
  • Cultural and school-environment sensitivity to adapt engagement style appropriately
  • Collaborative working style to partner effectively with teachers and Student Management staff
  • Alignment with care-based and restorative discipline principles to support student growth and development
  • Professional maturity, reliability, and respect for confidentiality and student dignity
  • Empathy and compassion demonstrated in youth work and student interactions

postedCompany.description:
Sunnyhill Ace Pte Ltd is a Singapore-based company primarily engaged in general cleaning services, including the cleaning of offices, public areas, and factories

The company's services encompass standard office cleaning duties as detailed in its job descriptions for cleaner positions.

Typical services provided by the company include cleaning and sanitizing, dusting, vacuuming, sweeping, trash removal, restocking supplies, equipment maintenance, and ensuring safety compliance.
postedCompany.name: SUNNYHILL ACE PTE. LTD.
